determine the time it will take for the car to reach the base of the tower after the angle of elevation changes from 45° to 60°, we can use trigonometry. \n\nLet's denote the height of the tower as \\( h \\) and the distance covered by the car during the observed time as \\( x \\).\n\nGiven:\n- Initial angle of elevation = 45°\n- Final angle of elevation = 60°\n- Time taken for the angle to change = 10 minutes\n\nUsing trigonometry, we can set up the following equation:\n\n\\( \\tan(60°) = \\frac{h}{x} \\)\n\nSolving for \\( x \\):\n\\( x = \\frac{h}{\\tan(60°)} \\)\n\nNow, we know that the car is moving at a uniform speed, the distance covered by the car during the observed time is proportional to the total distance to be covered:\n\n\\( \\frac{x}{10} = \\frac{h}{\\tan(60°)} \\times \\frac{1}{10} \\)\n\nThis equation represents the proportionality of the distance covered in 10 minutes to the total distance. \n\nWe can use this information to calculate the total time it will take for the car to reach the base of the tower after the angle of elevation changes from 45° to 60°. Let's calculate it."}],"parents":[0],"children":[2],"textembedding":[-0.09904554,-0.06464686,-0.03644247,-0.10583723,-0.041913558,0.051472235,0.011665364,0.12596077,0.0053649666,0.04244809,0.030185306,0.02855027,0.018394172,-0.049617097,-0.0064143776,0.019494677,0.0019927018,0.14639874,-0.05618869,0.10319602,0.019541841,-0.066847876,-0.01743516,0.03167885,0.0121055655,0.0038596317,-0.004370581,-0.0018905121,0.04685011,0.043642923,0.0038635621,0.023692321,0.0118383,-0.01693207,-0.057666514,0.06559016,0.10017749,-0.0011810787,-0.10910731,0.05782373,-0.067476735,0.06634479,-0.046755783,-0.050403174,0.011028642,-0.12728138,-0.07716119,-0.019746222,-0.020170702,-0.0056754667,-0.060905144,-0.122816466,0.06634479,0.077915825,0.0086153895,-0.016130274,0.04141047,0.14526679,-0.031851787,0.019573284,0.17306243,0.035499178,-0.11715672,0.014353742,0.023425056,-0.039901204,0.07653233,0.068923116,-0.08011684,-0.04839082,-0.04920834,-0.005034815,0.08212919,-0.044271782,0.035845052,-0.14778224,0.002596015,0.036285255,-0.0031128598,0.04373725,-0.11753404,0.011854022,-0.039083682,-0.010745655,0.008497478,0.0049090427,-0.07565193,-0.07225608,-0.024148246,0.038926467,-0.030295357,-0.07835603,0.024053916,-0.057289198,-0.08093435,0.035247635,0.0062532322,0.04807639,-0.12275358,0.041473355,0.09847956,0.10086923,0.02990232,0.019117361,0.03031108,-0.035876494,0.018755766,-0.020909613,0.04424034,0.022654701,-0.0046692896,-0.035687838,0.08741162,-0.055496946,-0.12470305,-0.045843937,0.120049484,-0.13860087,0.1430029,-0.00011797277,0.036316697,0.025909055,0.022984853,0.031348698,0.04663001,0.012750148,0.017419437,-0.037417203,0.07659522,-0.13218649,0.033612598,0.011358794,-0.057477854,-0.027434042,0.04892535,0.046472795,0.030373964,0.01795397,0.037417203,0.0022186986,-0.03128581,-0.07074682,-0.026254928,0.03905224,0.047604743,0.058955677,0.030782724,0.009794503,-0.08546215,0.101372324,-0.07829314,0.019337462,-0.033078067,-0.010863566,-0.018944424,-0.04027852,-0.10162386,0.02262326,-0.060307726,0.02771703,-0.0 substitute the values into the equation and calculate the total distance to be covered by the car:\n\n\\( x = h \\times \\frac{1}{\\tan(60°)} \\)\n\nFirst, we need to find the height of the tower (h). Let's assume a unit distance for this calculation. We can then calculate the total distance to be covered by the car after the angle of elevation after the angle of elevation changes from 45° to 60°.\n\nGiven:\n- Initial angle of elevation = 45°\n- Final angle of elevation = 60°\n\nWe previously calculated the height of the tower as \\( h =
